title: A Friendship That Grew from Being Neighbors

all.en: When my family first moved into our apartment building, an elderly woman named Mrs. Chen lived next door.
all.zh: 當我們家剛搬進這棟公寓時，隔壁住著一位名叫陳太太的年長女士。

all.en: On our first day, she knocked on our door with a plate of warm dumplings and a kind smile.
all.zh: 在我們搬來的第一天，她帶著一盤熱騰騰的水餃和親切的微笑來敲我們的門。

all.en: At first, I was just polite to her whenever we met in the hallway or elevator.
all.zh: 起初，我只是在走廊或電梯遇到她時對她保持禮貌。

all.en: However, one rainy afternoon, I helped her carry heavy grocery bags up three flights of stairs.
all.zh: 然而，在一個下雨的下午，我幫她把沉重的雜貨袋搬上三層樓的樓梯。

all.en: To thank me, she invited me in for tea and homemade pineapple cakes.
all.zh: 為了感謝我，她邀請我進去喝茶，並品嚐自製的鳳梨酥。

all.en: Her tiny living room was filled with old photographs and beautiful Chinese paintings.
all.zh: 她小小的客廳裡擺滿了舊照片和美麗的中國畫。

all.en: She told me stories about her youth, her late husband, and the city decades ago.
all.zh: 她跟我說了她年輕時的故事、她已故的丈夫，以及幾十年前的這座城市。

all.en: In return, I taught her how to use video calls so she could see her grandchildren overseas.
all.zh: 作為回報，我教她如何使用視訊通話，這樣她就能看到在海外的孫子們。

all.en: Soon, we developed a routine of having Sunday lunch together every weekend.
all.zh: 很快地，我們養成了每個週末一起吃星期天午餐的習慣。

all.en: She became like a grandmother to me, full of wisdom, humor, and gentle advice.
all.zh: 她對我來說就像一位祖母，充滿智慧、幽默和溫柔的建議。

all.en: Our unlikely friendship reminded me that family is not always defined by blood.
all.zh: 我們這段意想不到的友誼提醒了我，家人並非總是由血緣定義的。

all.en: Last winter, Mrs. Chen caught a bad cold and had to stay in bed for a whole week.
all.zh: 去年冬天，陳太太得了重感冒，必須臥床整整一週。

all.en: My mother cooked a pot of ginger chicken soup and asked me to bring it next door.
all.zh: 我媽媽煮了一鍋薑汁雞湯，要我送到隔壁去。

all.en: Mrs. Chen was so touched that she insisted on writing a thank-you card by hand.
all.zh: 陳太太非常感動，堅持要親手寫一張感謝卡。

all.en: After she recovered, she taught my mother how to make her famous radish cake.
all.zh: 她康復後，教我媽媽做她著名的蘿蔔糕。

all.en: The two of them now spend every Lunar New Year preparing dishes side by side.
all.zh: 她們倆現在每個農曆新年都並肩準備料理。

all.en: Her grandchildren finally visited from abroad and stayed in our building for a week.
all.zh: 她的孫子們終於從國外回來探望，並在我們的大樓住了一週。

all.en: They thanked me with a small wooden gift for taking such good care of their grandma.
all.zh: 他們以一個小小的木製禮物感謝我，謝謝我把他們的奶奶照顧得這麼好。

all.en: On her eightieth birthday, our whole floor gathered for a quiet candlelit celebration.
all.zh: 在她八十歲生日那天，我們整層樓的鄰居為她舉辦了一場安靜的燭光慶祝會。

all.en: She held my hand tightly and said that meeting our family was a true blessing.
all.zh: 她緊緊握住我的手，說認識我們一家是她真正的福氣。

all.en: Years from now, I will still remember how a plate of dumplings began everything.
all.zh: 多年以後，我仍會記得一切是如何從一盤水餃開始的。

all.en: The spring after her birthday, Mrs. Chen began teaching me her old family calligraphy style.
all.zh: 她生日後的那個春天，陳太太開始教我她家傳的書法風格。

all.en: Every Tuesday evening, I sat at her wooden table practicing strokes with a soft brush.
all.zh: 每個星期二晚上，我都坐在她的木桌前，用軟毛筆練習筆畫。

all.en: She patiently corrected my hand position and shared stories about her late father's lessons.
all.zh: 她耐心地調整我的執筆姿勢，並分享她已故父親當年教她的故事。

all.en: In return, I helped her digitize old letters and photographs onto a small laptop.
all.zh: 作為回報，我幫她把舊信件和照片數位化，儲存到一台小筆電裡。

all.en: Her grandchildren overseas were thrilled to receive scanned copies of family treasures.
all.zh: 她在海外的孫子們收到家族珍寶的掃描檔案時，都欣喜萬分。

all.en: One quiet Sunday, Mrs. Chen gave me her oldest calligraphy brush as a special gift.
all.zh: 在一個寧靜的星期天，陳太太把她最古老的書法毛筆送給我，作為一份特別的禮物。

all.en: She told me it had belonged to her father and now it should belong to me.
all.zh: 她告訴我那曾屬於她父親，如今應該屬於我。

all.en: I framed one of her handwritten poems and hung it proudly above my bedroom desk.
all.zh: 我把她親筆寫的一首詩裱框，驕傲地掛在我臥房書桌的上方。

all.en: When she passed away peacefully the following winter, our entire floor mourned together quietly.
all.zh: 當她在隔年冬天安詳離世時，我們整層樓的鄰居一起靜靜地哀悼。

all.en: Every dumpling I cook now carries the warmth of the neighbor who taught me family.
all.zh: 如今我煮的每一顆水餃，都帶著那位教我何謂家人的鄰居所傳遞的溫暖。
